issues.title: dom-runtime-parity guard was NOT extended for the FR2 corner-swap or the FR3 parallel-escape (plan promised it; the new runtime twins have no dedicated fixture)
issues.description: plan.md step 4 (line 194) and the Tests section (line 210) explicitly commit to 'extend dom-runtime-parity coverage' for the FR2/FR3 twins, and the project's own standards are emphatic about this exact recurring trap (coding-rules 'Keep the inlined vnmRuntime in parity ... extend it when you touch routing'; code-review-standards 'The dom-runtime-parity guard MUST cover the changed path'; flowchart-render-legibility REV-009 'each geometry CALL SITE that feeds the runtime needs its own parity guard, NOT just transitive coverage'). VERIFIED: test/dom-runtime-parity.test.ts is byte-for-byte unchanged vs master (git diff --stat empty); the only test file touched is test/geometry.test.ts, whose 4 new assertions exercise ONLY the geometry side (routeElbow / resolveLabelEdgeCollisions), never the runtime twin. I hand-verified the two runtime twins ARE currently byte-identical to their geometry originals (src/render/dom/runtime.ts perpendicularizeEntry L974-984 == src/geometry/index.ts L1042-1054 modulo nAt==n; the FR3 parallel branches L1477-1486 / L1497-1506 == geometry L611-622 / L634-644 modulo 6==PORT_LABEL_PAD), so there is NO live divergence today. But I also confirmed FR2 and FR3 changed ZERO snapshot-tested bytes (all four snapshot diffs are colour-only #8a93a6->#69728a), which means the fixes' actual output changes live ONLY in the un-snapshotted gallery/hero PNGs (assets/example-dark.png, docs/*). So neither the new FR2 swap nor the FR3 parallel branch is locked in by any static-vs-runtime byte-compare; a future edit to either twin (or to the elbowThrough call site) could silently drift the interactive/HTML-export view with the whole suite green - the precise class this project has re-opened multiple times (REV-003/REV-007/REV-009, hybrid TEST-003).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Add a dom-runtime-parity fixture that GENUINELY triggers both new branches and byte-compares runtime==static: (a) a diagram with a left/right-exit -> top/bottom-entry edge whose naive/threaded elbow closes parallel to the entered border (the idle->Loading shape) so perpendicularizeEntry actually flips a corner, asserting the swap fired (last segment perpendicular) and edgePaths(root) === expectedPaths(...); (b) a labelled edge whose plate is bisected by a foreign PARALLEL run (the 'give up' shape) driven through toSvgString()==renderSvg so the FR3 parallel slide is exercised in the twin. Bite-verify each by confirming the fixture would fail if one twin's branch were reverted (mirrors the REV-009 approach).
issues.fix_summary: Added dom-runtime-parity test 'toSvgString() == renderSvg for the state-machine fixture (FR2 ⟂-entry + FR3 parallel-label)' — the state-machine DSL drives BOTH new branches (idle→Loading perpendicularizeEntry swap + give-up parallel-escape); it byte-compares the runtime serializer to renderSvg (bite-verified: reverting either twin branch diverges the two), with two sanity asserts proving the static side genuinely fires each branch (idle→Loading closes vertical into the top; give-up labelPos moved off its naive midpoint). 397 unit tests green.

issues.title: perpendicularizeEntry assumes the pre-corner point lies OUTSIDE the entered border - no guard that the flipped closing segment approaches from outside the node (latent, not observed in the corpus)
issues.description: src/geometry/index.ts perpendicularizeEntry (L1042-1054) + its runtime twin swap the last elbow corner to out[len-2] = entryVertical ? {x:end.x, y:a.y} : {x:a.x, y:end.y}. The new closing segment is b'->end with b' at (end.x, a.y) (top/bottom entry) or (a.x, end.y) (side entry). This enters perpendicular ONLY if the pre-corner point a=out[len-3] sits on the OUTSIDE of the entered border (a.y < end.y for a top entry / a.y > end.y for a bottom entry / a.x on the exterior side for a left/right entry). The function checks finalPerp and a non-degeneracy 'swappable' guard (a.y!==end.y / a.x!==end.x) but NOT that a is exterior. If a ever fell on the interior side, the flipped segment would approach the border from INSIDE the node (arrowhead sitting on the border but the line running through the node body). VERIFIED this does NOT materialise: the swap only fires when the final segment is parallel to the border, which for a genuine top/bottom (or side) entry only happens with an exterior approach (port assignment routes a top-entry from above); every static snapshot is byte-identical (the swap fires on none of them) and the regenerated heroes were eyeballed (idle->Loading, prod/fail->Done all enter cleanly). Reported as an unguarded invariant of a new helper, not a demonstrated defect - the same latent-but-unobserved class this project logs as a nit (cf. flowchart-render-legibility REV-004/REV-005).
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE (optional, low priority; no change needed to ship). Either add an explicit exterior guard - only swap when a is on the outside of the entered border (entryVertical: (entrySide==='top' ? a.y < end.y : a.y > end.y); analogous for left/right) - mirrored byte-for-byte in the runtime twin; or document the exterior-approach invariant in the helper's doc-comment and add a geometry unit case for a bottom-entry (entryVertical with the target ABOVE the source) to prove the sign handling. Keep both twins in lockstep (nAt==n) so parity holds.
issues.fix_summary: Chose the low-risk option (no behaviour change): documented the exterior-approach invariant in perpendicularizeEntry's doc-comment (why a parallel closing segment only arises from an exterior approach, so the flipped corner always enters from outside) and added a geometry unit test 'handles a BOTTOM entry too — closes vertical, approaching from below (sign check)' proving the sign handling for a bottom entry (target above source). The explicit exterior guard was declined as it would require threading the entry Side through the helper + both twins for a not-observed case (contra smallest-correct-change).

issues.title: FR3 parallel-escape slide target is not clamped to the label's own-run extent - a long co-extensive bisecting parallel run could slide the label far along/off its own edge (continuation of REV-008; not observed)
issues.description: The new FR3 branch in resolveLabelEdgeCollisions (src/geometry/index.ts L611-622 / L634-644 + runtime twin) escapes a bisecting foreign PARALLEL run by accumulating hiTarget = hi + hw + PORT_LABEL_PAD and loTarget = lo - hw - PORT_LABEL_PAD, where [lo,hi] is the FOREIGN run's bounded extent, then sliding the label along its own axis to the nearer of the two (tie -> +axis). Like the pre-existing perpendicular case (flagged for the prior feature as REV-008 'label-vs-edge slide has no clamp to its own run's extent'), these targets are NOT clamped to the LABEL'S OWN run interval. For a foreign parallel run that is long and roughly co-extensive with the label's own run (both ends distant), the nearer-end escape can still be a large move that carries the plate centre toward or past the end of its own edge segment (the label floats off the line it labels) and outward past the viewBox (cf. REV-004). Determinism is fine (fixed edge x edge x segment order, max/min accumulation, capped at LABEL_EDGE_PASSES=4, no RNG) and the combined perpendicular+parallel hi/lo accumulation is coherent. VERIFIED clean on the target case: the give-up label lifts a short, sensible distance and reads as one word in the regenerated assets/example-dark.png, and every regenerated light gallery label sits on its line. Reported as a latent limitation extending REV-008 to the parallel case, not a demonstrated defect.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION (low priority, no change needed to ship). If a hard guarantee is wanted later, clamp the parallel-escape target to the label's own-run interval [lo_own+hw, hi_own-hw] (falling back to the smaller residual overlap when the own run is too short), applied identically in geometry and the runtime twin, and/or fold the label plate into contentBounds (the REV-004 enclosure) so an outward slide can never clip. This is the same deferred own-run-clamp follow-up already tracked as flowchart-render-legibility REV-008 - now also covering the FR3 parallel branch.
issues.fix_summary: DEFERRED (documented known-limitation). This is explicitly a continuation of flowchart-render-legibility REV-008 (label-vs-edge slide own-run clamp) — an already-user-accepted deferral — and the reviewer marked it 'no change needed to ship', verified not-observed. Adding the clamp now (to both twins) is out of this feature's minimal scope; carried forward as the existing own-run-clamp follow-up, now also covering the FR3 parallel branch. Surfaced to the user in the report's known-limitations.
